# Test Review — ai_chat/ui/widgets

**Scope:** `lib/features/ai_chat/ui/widgets/` + `test/features/ai_chat/ui/widgets/`
**Generated by automated test-quality review (static analysis)**

---

---

## File size / split opportunities

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `input_area.dart` at 512 lines is the most oversized widget file. Natural split seams:
  - `_TranscriptionProgressIndicator` widget (processing + partial transcript UI) → `transcription_progress.dart` (~60 ln)
  - `ChatVoiceControls` widget (recording state, waveform, cancel/stop) → already partially split since `chat_voice_controls_test.dart` exists separately, but the impl is still in `input_area.dart`. Extract it fully.
  - Core `InputArea` (text field, send/mic/tune button selection) → `input_area.dart` (~300 ln)
  - The test would mirror the split: `input_area_test.dart` (1138 ln) contains both `InputArea` and `ChatVoiceControls` tests, which already have a separate file (`chat_voice_controls_test.dart`). Consolidate and deduplicate.
- [ ] **[MED]** `chat_interface_test.dart` at 722 lines is large for a 118-line widget. The test groups (empty state, error, streaming, model-selection, send-message, new-chat) could be split into `chat_interface_empty_state_test.dart` and `chat_interface_send_test.dart` but this would violate the one-file-per-source rule. More practical: extract the `_createSession` / `_stubRepository` / `_pumpChatInterface` helpers to a shared `test/features/ai_chat/ui/widgets/chat_interface_test_utils.dart`.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `thinking_parser.dart` at 446 lines is near the 500-line boundary. No split needed yet.

---

## Test quality improvements

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `chat_interface_test.dart` line 118: `GetIt.instance.pushNewScope()` / `GetIt.instance.registerSingleton` / `GetIt.instance.resetScope()` / `GetIt.instance.popScope()` is manual GetIt management. Use `setUpTestGetIt()` / `tearDownTestGetIt()` from `test/widget_test_utils.dart`.
- [x] **[HIGH]** Two test files cover `thinking_parser.dart`:
  - `test/features/ai_chat/ui/widgets/thinking_parser_test.dart` (495 ln)
  - `test/features/ai_chat/ui/widgets/chat_interface/thinking_parser_test.dart` (292 ln)
  This violates the "one test file per source file" rule. The second file must be merged into the first or deleted. Check for coverage overlap and consolidate.
- [ ] **[HIGH]** `chat_interface_test.dart` has 29 `pumpAndSettle` calls. Many of these are used after operations that produce no animation (e.g., after tapping a button that triggers a Riverpod state change). Replace with `tester.pump()` + `tester.pump(duration)` for specific animations. The "error banner close" test at line 210 and "retry" test at line 251 both use `pumpAndSettle` where a simple provider state update occurs — use `tester.pump()` instead.
- [ ] **[MED]** `chat_interface/input_area_test.dart` line 21: `_MockRealtimeService extends Mock implements RealtimeTranscriptionService` is defined inline. If `MockRealtimeTranscriptionService` exists in `test/mocks/mocks.dart`, this is a violation. Check and consolidate.
- [ ] **[MED]** `chat_interface/assistant_settings_sheet_test.dart`: `_pumpSheet` uses `await tester.pumpAndSettle()` to wait for the `FutureProvider` (`eligibleChatModelsForCategoryProvider`) to resolve. Since this is a mocked async provider returning immediately, `await tester.pump()` + `await tester.pump()` is sufficient.
- [ ] **[MED]** `messages_area_test.dart` lines 40–41 and 67–68: assertions `findsWidgets` on `AnimatedBuilder` and `findsWidgets` on `Row` are weak per "findsOneWidget alone is not a valid test" rule. The first should assert that `TypingIndicator` is present (use `find.byType(TypingIndicator)` or check the number of dots). The second should find the trailing row specifically (e.g., by key or check bubble count).
- [ ] **[MED]** `chat_header_test.dart`: only one test, covering title display and settings sheet open. Missing: `canClearChat: true` shows a clear button, `isStreaming: true` disables the settings button (or shows loading state), `onClearChat` / `onNewSession` callbacks are invoked. These are the most interesting behavioral branches.
- [ ] **[MED]** `message_bubble_test.dart` line 62: "shows copy action for assistant visible content" taps the copy button and adds the comment "We don't assert the SnackBar (theme-dependent). The tap should not throw." This is a no-assertion test body — the tap must not throw is not a meaningful assertion. At minimum, verify that the clipboard was written or that `find.byType(DesignSystemToast)` / `find.text(...)` appears.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `chat_interface/chat_voice_controls_test.dart` line 11: `_FakeRecorderController extends ChatRecorderController` defined inline. If a shared fake exists, use it; otherwise note for future extraction.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `streaming_content_test.dart` line 1 uses `pumpAndSettle` once — acceptable since the `ThinkingDisclosure` animation settles on first open.

---

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

- [ ] **[MED]** `thinking_parser.dart` (446 lines) contains complex stateful parsing with 5+ token types. The Glados test in `thinking_parser_test.dart` likely covers round-trip parsing invariants. Verify there is a chunking-invariant property (parse-as-one == parse-in-N-chunks) similar to `chat_stream_parser_test.dart`. If not, add one. The parser is pure logic and an ideal Glados target.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `waveform_bars.dart`: `_WaveformBarsPainter.shouldRepaint` is a pure function comparing old vs. new `List<double>` + `double` parameters. A Glados property — `shouldRepaint` is `true` iff any parameter differs — would catch regression on the equality check.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `message_timestamp.dart`: the time-formatting logic (`HH:mm`) is trivial but could benefit from a Glados property: for any `DateTime`, the formatted string has exactly 5 chars (`HH:mm`), hours ∈ [00..23], minutes ∈ [00..59]. Current static tests cover only two concrete timestamps.
- [ ] **[LOW]** Widget tests are generally poor Glados candidates per guidelines. The pure-logic components above are the exception.

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `bubble_corner_action.dart` (36 lines) has no dedicated test. The widget is tested only indirectly through `message_bubble_test.dart`. Add `bubble_corner_action_test.dart` covering: renders icon, tooltip visible, `onTap` callback invoked, and that the circular Material border is applied.
- [ ] **[HIGH]** `error_banner.dart` (59 lines) has no dedicated test. Tested only indirectly through the full `ChatInterface`. Add `error_banner_test.dart` covering: displays error text, Retry button triggers `onRetry`, Close button triggers `onDismiss`.
- [ ] **[MED]** `typing_indicator.dart` (69 lines) has no direct test. It is verified only via `messages_area_test.dart` as `find.byType(AnimatedBuilder)`. Add a minimal `typing_indicator_test.dart`: renders 3 dots for `isUser: false`, renders different colors for `isUser: true`, animation controller starts on `initState` and is disposed on `dispose`.
- [ ] **[MED]** `input_area.dart` transcript-listener path (lines 46–62): when `ChatRecorderState.transcript` arrives and `canSend == false`, the text is placed in the controller rather than sent. `input_area_test.dart` tests the `canSend == true` path; the `canSend == false` + transcript path should also be tested.
- [ ] **[MED]** `ai_chat_icon_test.dart`: `pumpAndSettle` verifies the modal appears (`findsOneWidget` on `ChatInterface`) but does not verify the `categoryId` passed to `ChatInterface` matches the journal page's selected category. This is the core logic being tested and the assertion is missing.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `chat_interface/thinking_disclosure.dart`: tests cover expand/collapse with `pumpAndSettle`. Missing: the "collapsed by default" initial state (the first `pumpAndSettle` verifies the collapsed state, but only if the test actually reads the initial widget tree before expansion).

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `chat_interface_test.dart` has 29 `pumpAndSettle` calls. Most operations are Riverpod state changes with no animation. Replacing them with `tester.pump()` eliminates potential 10-second hangs if any provider/animation doesn't settle (e.g., if the `_AmbientPulseBorder` repeating animation were accidentally mounted). Impact: each avoidable `pumpAndSettle` risks the 10-second timeout.
- [ ] **[HIGH]** `input_area.dart` line 87: `Future.delayed(const Duration(milliseconds: 100), ...)` in production `_sendMessage` forces every test that exercises "send and check cleared text" to pay 100ms real-wait. The tests at lines 878–882 and 905–910 comment "Drain the 100ms Future.delayed" and use `tester.pump(const Duration(milliseconds: 200))`. This real wait is repeated per test. Refactor `_sendMessage` to accept an injectable scroll delay (or use `WidgetsBinding.instance.addPostFrameCallback`) so tests can avoid the 100ms wait.
- [ ] **[MED]** `chat_interface/assistant_settings_sheet_test.dart` has 8 `pumpAndSettle` calls. The initial `pumpAndSettle` in `_pumpSheet` waits for a mocked FutureProvider to resolve — replace with bounded `pump()` calls.
- [ ] **[MED]** `message_bubble_test.dart` has 5 `pumpAndSettle` calls on a widget containing `GptMarkdown` (which may spawn indefinite animations). Use `tester.pump()` instead for tests that only need layout to complete.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `chat_interface/thinking_disclosure_test.dart` has 4 `pumpAndSettle` calls to expand the disclosure panel. The expand animation has a finite duration — use `tester.pump(const Duration(milliseconds: 300))` for determinism.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `chat_interface/chat_interface_thinking_test.dart` has 9 `pumpAndSettle` calls, many of which drive streaming setup. Where a `StreamController` is involved, prefer `tester.pump()` after `streamController.add(...)` and `await tester.pump()` after `streamController.close()`.

---

## Summary

- `input_area.dart` at 512 lines is the most oversized widget impl; `ChatVoiceControls` is already separately tested but not yet extracted from the impl file.
- Two test files cover `thinking_parser.dart` — a direct violation of the one-test-file-per-source rule that must be resolved by merging.
- 29 `pumpAndSettle` calls in `chat_interface_test.dart` represent the largest speed risk; the repeating `_AmbientPulseBorder` animation makes this a correctness risk as well.
- Two widget files (`bubble_corner_action.dart`, `error_banner.dart`) have no dedicated test; `typing_indicator.dart` has only indirect coverage.
- Best Glados opportunity: a chunking-invariant property for `thinking_parser.dart` if not already present.
- 3 speed wins: eliminating the 100ms real-delay from `InputArea._sendMessage`, replacing `pumpAndSettle` with bounded `pump` in 5+ test files, and consolidating duplicate `thinking_parser_test.dart` files.
